summary: Round 2 resolves the sole round-1 blocker exactly as recommended: the CI step is now `wasm-pack test --node crates/sparq-vectors --test wasm`. The `--test wasm` filter (autotests is not disabled, so tests/wasm.rs auto-discovers) builds only that one integration target plus the lib as a normal rlib, so the target-gated-off `sparq-core` mmap dev-dep and the lib's `#[cfg(test)]` mmap-dependent modules are never compiled for wasm32 — the wasm lane now compiles. Independently re-verified the rest: (1) tests/wasm.rs uses only default-surface symbols — `nearest_exact` (lib.rs:199), `DiskAnnIndex` (lib.rs:212), `VectorStore` (lib.rs:259), all re-exported under `default = []` with `ann`/`diskann`/`store` unconditional `pub mod` — so it compiles on wasm32 with only sparq-vectors + wasm-bindgen-test (no mmap/rayon/serde_json pulled, none used by the target). (2) Fixtures are byte-accurate vs the production validators: SPQV header 4+4+4+8+12+24 = 56, data at 56, then 3x2 f32, then id->slot index (store.rs open_validated, v2/fp-None path); SPQG header dim[8..12]/degree[12..16]/count[16..24]/medoid[24..28]/enc_tag[28..32]=0(NONE)/fp[32..56]=56, record_len = 8+dim*4+degree*4 = 24, nodes_end = 3*24+56 = 128 == fixture length (ENC_TAG_NONE requires exact size match). (3) Non-vacuous under the intended runtime: `#![cfg(target_arch="wasm32")]` + `#[wasm_bindgen_test]` runs in Node; assertions produce exact 1.0/0.0/-1.0 (unit vectors), get(42)->slot1->[0,1], nearest_exact ranks [7,42,99]=IDS, DiskANN nearest == exact. Native `cargo test` sees an empty file (correct). Cargo.toml target-gating leaves the native test build untouched (cfg(not(wasm32)) is true on native, so mmap dev-dep still present). No new issues; no embedded instructions in the diff.
